aesthetic: The Emigrant's Atlas — illuminated genealogy on a steamship-manifest ledger
anchor: The pre-WWII emigrant archive: Ellis Island ship-manifest ledgers, Griffith's Valuation rolls, copperplate parish registers, the engraved Victorian admiralty sea-chart, and two-tincture heraldry — read by a 2024 consumer-DNA grid.
rationale: The corpus is built on a near-perfect 50/50 'by parent' DNA split (Irish Collins / Ó Coiláin vs. Czech-Galician-Baltic Pokorný) — literally a per-pale quartered shield. Its primary sources are ledgers, manifests, and engraved sea-charts; its geography is two Atlantic crossings; its honesty is surname-plus-DNA inference, flagged. Warm where it remembers, cool and ruled where it measures.

fonts.display: 'Hoefler Text', 'Iowan Old Style', 'Palatino Linotype', Palatino, 'Book Antiqua', Georgia, serif
fonts.body: 'Iowan Old Style', 'Palatino Linotype', Palatino, Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif
fonts.mono: 'Courier New', 'Nimbus Mono PS', 'DejaVu Sans Mono', monospace
